content.problemStatement:
You are given 333 integers LLL, RRR and XXX.

Consider the set S={LâŠ•X,(L+1)âŠ•X,...,RâŠ•X}S = \{L \oplus X, (L + 1) \oplus X, ... ,R \oplus X\}S={LâŠ•X,(L+1)âŠ•X,...,RâŠ•X}, where âŠ•\oplusâŠ• represents the Bitwise XOR operator.

Find the MEXâ€ ^{\dagger}â€  value of this set SSS.

â€ ^{\dagger}â€  The MEX of a set SSS is the minimum non-negative integer which is not present in SSS.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first and only line of each test case contains 333 integers - LLL, RRR and XXX.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the MEX value of the set SSS.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The set is {1âŠ•2}={3}\{1 \oplus 2\} = \{3\}{1âŠ•2}={3}. Since 000 does not belong to this set, it is the MEX of this set.
Test Case 2 : We can prove that each of 000 to 999 is a part of the set SSS, but 101010 is not. Thus, it's mex is 101010.
